content: This is a standard slide facing the camera. When you press Space, the lerp pathing will pull the camera straight THROUGH the geometry of this slide to reach the next one.
~ Because the Data Spikes and Asteroids share the same Z-Buffer as the blitted text, watch how the floating objects gracefully clip through the black font as you move!
You are now looking backward. The camera flew through the first slide.
If you look behind you (or press Backspace), you will see the back of Slide 1.
Because of the `abs(dot_val)` hack in Render Pass 1, this slide is perfectly lit even though its normal vector is pointing directly away from the sun source. The engine architecture holds firm.
This slide is rotated exactly 90 degrees.
To the global sun vector, the surface area is zero. It should be pitch black.

However, the `headlamp` dot product calculation compares the slide's normal vector to the Camera's Forward Vector (`cfw`). By merely looking at this slide, your camera illuminates it, proving the Two-Sided Headlamp shader works from every possible angle.
